title: [SUPERSEDED — duplicate PROP-065 authoring caused by FUSE-staleness on operator-directive status]
author: tinker
summary: DO NOT READ AS A SUBSTANTIVE PROPOSAL. This file was authored at 05:42 in good faith by tinker-2026-05-31T05-42 in response to DIRECTIVE-20260531-001 (pending status read from FUSE). At Mode 0 directive-discovery time, FUSE's copy of the directive file showed status='pending' and no completion fields. After authoring the full PROP (42KB) and cloning fresh to push, the clone-side directive file showed status='completed', completed_at='2026-05-31T05:35:00Z', completed_by_run='tinker-2026-05-31T05-23', prop_id_authored='PROP-065'. The PROP had ALREADY been authored at commit 65f80e9 by the immediately-prior tinker run (05-23, 12 minutes earlier). My authored content was not pushed; this stub is a redirect so the FUSE→git round-trip doesn't add a duplicate 42KB PROP to the repo.

root_cause_of_the_duplicate.diagnosis: FUSE-staleness on operator-directive status, the same PROP-061/064 silent-skip surface. The prior tinker run committed the directive lifecycle update (status: pending → completed) at 05:35Z. PROP-061/064 sync-workspace should have propagated the new file content to FUSE within the hour but did not — workspace-sync's Step 4c LOCAL==REMOTE early-return branch suppressed the sync (PROP-064 Bug A). My run at 05:42Z (7 minutes after the prior commit) read FUSE's stale 'pending' status and re-picked the directive.
root_cause_of_the_duplicate.instrumentation_evidence: FUSE directive file status='pending' at my run start; clone directive file status='completed' on git fetch. Side-by-side at the moment of detection: see this run's report findings.
root_cause_of_the_duplicate.this_is_the_canary_event_for_prop_064: DIRECTIVE-20260531-002 (already-pending, asks for deeper PROP-064 silent-skip fix) is directly validated by this duplicate-authoring event. The harm pattern is no longer hypothetical — staleness on monitor/tinker/operator-directives/ files causes tinker to do duplicate work on already-completed asks.
root_cause_of_the_duplicate.next_priority_directive_for_a_future_tin: DIRECTIVE-20260531-002 should ship before the next operator-directive update cycle to prevent further duplicate-authoring incidents.

what_my_proposal_would_have_added: Same diagnosis as the existing PROP-065 with a slightly different recommended path: my draft proposed reusing the existing NEVER_PUSH + OWNERSHIP-git pattern (directive's option iv) rather than introducing a new GIT_APPEND_ONLY ownership category (existing PROP-065's option_ii). The two approaches are functionally equivalent for the affected files; the existing PROP-065 is more thorough (15 files in scope vs my 4; 60-day audit identifying 8 total lost rows across 2 events vs my 1; per-file recovery recommendations). Operator should adopt the existing PROP-065 as authored.
operator_action_recommended: Either delete this file from FUSE (currently blocked by allow_cowork_file_delete rejection in the parent session — operator can grant if desired) OR leave as a redirect stub for audit-trail purposes. No proposal action needed.
